description_long: This is a tall, rectangular clock tower constructed from weathered, dark brown wood, giving it a rustic and aged appearance. The front face features a circular clock with black Arabic numerals and two simple black hands, set into a recessed panel. Below the clock face, there are three vertical glass or transparent panels, possibly for viewing internal mechanisms or decorative elements. The sides and back of the tower are plain, with subtle paneling and a slightly protruding base and top, adding to its architectural solidity. The top of the tower is flat with raised edges, resembling a parapet or battlement, and the overall structure is robust and monolithic. The surface shows signs of wear and patina, suggesting long-term exposure to the elements.
description: A weathered, wooden clock tower with a circular clock face and vertical glass panels on the front, featuring a flat, parapet-style roof and a rustic, aged finish.
description_view_0: The front view displays the circular clock face with black numerals and hands, as well as three vertical glass panels beneath it.
description_view_1: The left side view shows a plain, flat wooden surface with no visible features or openings.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a solid wooden panel with subtle horizontal detailing and no clock face or glass panels.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, with a plain, uninterrupted wooden surface.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock face is circular with black Arabic numerals (1-12) and two simple black hands for hours and minutes. The face is set into a recessed panel with no additional decorative elements or markings, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
